query: MongoDB sharding write throughput performance 50000 writes per second benchmark
answer: MongoDB can achieve up to 50,000 writes per second with proper sharding and architecture design. Performance depends on shard key choice and cluster configuration. Scaling beyond this requires horizontal sharding and optimized hardware.

results.title: Scaling MongoDB to 100K+ Writes per Second
results.snippet: DATA, AI, AND THE REAL WORLD. # Scaling MongoDB to 100K+ Writes per Second. Sustaining 100K+ writes per second in MongoDB is not a tuning trick — it is an architectural decision. This article breaks down how to design a sharded cluster using realistic Atlas hardware (32GB RAM, 8 CPU, standard storage) and achieve linear horizontal scaling through deterministic shard key distribution, clean write paths, and disciplined index strategy. > Scaling MongoDB to 100K Writes per Second is not a tuning exercise — it is an architectural decision. This article breaks down how to design a sharded cluster using realistic Atlas hardware (32GB RAM, 8 CPU, standard storage) and achieve linear horizontal scaling through deterministic shard key distribution, clean write…. ## Why Scaling MongoDB to 100K Writes per Second Requires Horizontal Sharding ? Scaling MongoDB to 100K writes per second cannot be achieved with vertical upgrades alone because a single node—even with generous RAM, CPU, and I/O—quickly reaches physical limits.

results.title: MongoDB write performance - Working with Data
results.snippet: # MongoDB write performance. I’m using the Spark connector (10.3.0) to copy data from BigQuery to Mongo. Beside the index on `_id: ObjectID`, there is one compound index on two fields. When I tried to use “insert” only, with a M10 cluster, sometimes I got 10+K inserted documents per second, but most of the time I got only ~2K/sec. When I tried to use “replace + upsert”, I usually got 0.6-0.7K/sec. When the performance was bad (~2K), CPU IOWAIT was usually high, between 80-130%. I tried to scale the cluster up to M30. Despite CPU IOWAIT reduced to about 50%, the performance hadn’t improved. What is the typical bulk write speed should I expect from a M10 or M30 cluster? P/S: I’m using serverless GCP DataProc to run Spark. There’s hardly any transformation needed - just read from BigQuery and write to Mongo. I tried to use one single index (the one on `_id`), the performance increased by a few percents.

results.title: Benchmarking MongoDB multi-document transactions in a sharded cluster
results.snippet: Benchmarking MongoDB multi-document transactions in a sharded cluster by Tushar Panpaliya Relational databases like Oracle, MySQL, and Microsoft SQL Server offer trans-action processing as an integral part of their design. We have adapted pytpcc framework to conduct a series of benchmarking experi-ments aiming at finding the impact of tunable consistency, database size, and design choices on the multi-document transaction in MongoDB sharded clusters. Multi-document transactions with ACID guarantees are available in MongoDB driver version 4.2 across multiple operations, collections, databases, and shards. snapshot where snapshot provides the most consistent data for read operations of multi-document transactions running across the shards. 35 Result Analysis - Results from experiment 3 suggest that MongoDB sharding can improve throughput, working with huge data while complying with ACID guarantees for the multi-document transactions. Our experiments in benchmarking multi-document transactions in the sharded cluster using industry-standard benchmark like TPC-C gave us valuable insights about read-write consistency, system scalability, and schema design choices for MongoDB.
